stem: What creates vessel-to-background contrast in time-of-flight MR angiography?

options.text: Gadolinium selectively shortens blood T1 during every TOF acquisition
Stationary tissue receives fewer RF pulses than blood entering the slab
Velocity-dependent phase subtraction makes inflowing blood bright
Fresh inflowing blood is less saturated than stationary tissue

explanation: A short-TR gradient-echo sequence repeatedly saturates tissue within the slab. Spins flowing into it arrive with more longitudinal magnetization and therefore appear bright.

stem: Which condition can make a patent vessel appear falsely dark on TOF MRA?

options.text: Slow in-plane flow that becomes saturated in the imaging slab
Rapid perpendicular inflow that continually supplies fresh spins
A saturation band correctly placed over only undesired venous inflow
A short-TR gradient echo with rapid replacement of blood spins

explanation: Blood that remains in the slab for many RF pulses loses the inflow advantage and becomes saturated. Slow flow and flow parallel to the slab are therefore vulnerable to signal loss.

stem: Why can two-dimensional TOF be advantageous for slow flow over a long anatomic region?

options.text: A thick three-dimensional slab prevents repeated excitation of slow blood
Thin independently excited slices limit saturation of entering spins
Two-dimensional TOF avoids RF excitation of both blood and stationary tissue
Two-dimensional TOF measures slow velocity through phase subtraction

explanation: Each thin 2D slice has a short transit distance, reducing saturation of slowly moving spins. Three-dimensional TOF offers higher spatial resolution but is more susceptible to saturation across a thick slab.

stem: How can a saturation band help isolate arterial flow in TOF imaging?

options.text: Place it on the arterial inflow side to increase arterial saturation
Apply it to every voxel after reconstruction to remove background
Place it on the venous inflow side to suppress entering venous spins
Use it to alter the gyromagnetic ratio of blood entering from one direction

explanation: A directional saturation band pre-saturates spins entering from the undesired direction. Placement must reflect flow direction so the target arterial inflow remains fresh.

stem: What does a maximum-intensity projection do to a TOF volume?

options.text: Calculates ADC from multiple b-values
Subtracts phase in stationary tissue only
Converts all dark tissue to a gadolinium concentration
Displays the highest-signal voxel along each viewing ray

explanation: MIP compresses a three-dimensional bright-vessel dataset into projection images by selecting maximum voxel intensity. Overlap or bright background structures can obscure vessels, so source images remain important.
